vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The visual data indicates a severe discrepancy between the registry and the physical state of the biome. The pots are present, but the biological specimens are largely absent or non-viable.

inventory_reconciliation.P1 (Yellow Pot): Systemic Loss: No visible plant matter.
inventory_reconciliation.P2 (Black Pot): Systemic Loss: No visible plant matter.
inventory_reconciliation.P3 (Black Pot): Systemic Loss: The white rabbit anchor is present, but the Pothos is absent.
inventory_reconciliation.P4 (Black Pot): Systemic Loss: No visible plant matter.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil surface; no signs of String of Nickels.
plant_audit.P2: Empty soil surface; no signs of Mexican Mint.
plant_audit.P3: Empty soil surface; white rabbit anchor remains, but host plant is missing.
plant_audit.P4: Empty soil surface; no signs of Silver Guest.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Appears dark and potentially saturated, but lacks any organic growth.
biome_observations.debris: White particulate matter (likely perlite or eggshell fragments) is visible in the black pots, but no plant tissue.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean, no biological debris or fungal blooms detected.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-2: Minimal change; pots appear empty in both frames.
temporal_deltas.t-2_to_t-1: Significant light degradation; the environment has shifted to a low-light/blue-spectrum state, obscuring detail but confirming the absence of foliage.
temporal_deltas.t-1_to_current: Static state; no growth or recovery observed.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently in a state of total biological failure. There is no evidence of living plant tissue in any of the four designated pots. The environment is effectively a collection of empty containers.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of the white rabbit anchor in an otherwise empty pot (P3) is the only non-soil feature. No new introductions or incidental growth detected.
vision_report.narrative_description: Upon meticulous audit of the provided sequence, I have determined that the biome has suffered a complete systemic loss of all registered specimens. The pots, which should contain P1-P4, are devoid of any visible plant life. The lighting conditions have deteriorated across the sequence, further confirming the lack of photosynthetic activity. The white rabbit anchor in P3 remains the only constant, highlighting the absence of the Pothos that should be accompanying it. The current state is one of total dormancy or death.
